steps.title: Set up the equation from the problem statement
steps.content: The table gives group A as $375$ objects and group C as $690$ objects. The statement “C is $p\%$ of A” means $$690=\frac{p}{100}\cdot375.$$

steps.title: Use the percent shortcut
steps.content: To find what percent $690$ is of $375$, compute $$\frac{690}{375}\cdot100=1.84\cdot100=184$$ So group C has $184\%$ as many objects as group A.
